skill.name: lint
skill.description: Provides project-specific linting, auto-fix, and typecheck command guidance for code verification workflows.
skill.summary: Run project lint and typecheck commands during code changes.
skill.author: Clouder0

security_audit.summary: The four Ruby backtick findings are false positives because the backticks are Markdown code fences, not Ruby execution. The skill still authorizes Bash and documents npx lint and typecheck commands, so external command execution is a real but expected medium-risk capability. The weak cryptographic algorithm finding at SKILL.md line 3 is a false positive; no cryptographic behavior, prompt injection, network access, or exfiltration evidence was found.

medium_findings.title: Bash Lint And Typecheck Commands
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE for external command capability, but FALSE POSITIVE for Ruby backtick execution. SKILL.md declares Bash access and shows lint, auto-fix, and typecheck commands. This is expected for a lint skill, but it can execute local project tooling and change files when auto-fix is used.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file clearly declares Bash access and includes concrete npx lint and typecheck commands. Confidence is high for external command risk, while the Ruby backtick interpretation is dismissed because the backticks are Markdown fences.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. The reported line is front matter description for linting and typechecking. No evidence found of cryptographic APIs, algorithm names, hashing, encryption, credential handling, or weak cryptography in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line contains only a human-readable description. The full skill file contains no cryptographic code or instructions.

low_findings.title: Auto-fix May Modify Source Files
low_findings.description: The documented auto-fix command is legitimate for linting, but it may rewrite user files. This is an operational risk, not evidence of malicious intent.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The auto-fix behavior is explicit in the lint command block. It is a normal linting feature, but users should understand that it can change files.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Execution Through Bash
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill enables Bash and documents npx-based commands for linting and typechecking. These commands are appropriate for the stated purpose, but they execute local project tools and should run only in trusted workspaces.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools metadata and command examples directly support this pattern. No network access, obfuscation, or unauthorized command intent was found.

content.user_title: Run Project Linting and Type Checks
content.value_statement: Code changes need repeatable lint and typecheck verification before review. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code concise project commands for linting, auto-fix, and type checking.
actual_capabilities: Provides example ESLint commands for checking selected files.
Shows an auto-fix command for lint issues that support fixes.
Provides a TypeScript no-emit typecheck command.
Lists common lint issues such as import order and unused variables.
Mentions alternative placeholders for Python and Go lint workflows.
limitations: Does not detect the actual package manager or project configuration.
Requires project dependencies and lint tools to be installed separately.
Includes template commands that may need adjustment for each repository.
Auto-fix commands can modify files and should be reviewed afterward.

use_cases.title: Verify Code Before Review
use_cases.description: Run lint and typecheck commands after implementing a change.

use_cases.title: Clean Up Style Issues
use_cases.description: Use auto-fix guidance to resolve import order and formatting issues.

use_cases.title: Standardize Agent Verification
use_cases.description: Give coding agents a consistent verification checklist for pull requests.

prompt_templates.title: Run Basic Lint
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the lint skill to check the files I changed and summarize any issues.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner verification after a small edit.

prompt_templates.title: Fix Lint Issues
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the lint skill to run the auto-fix command, then report what changed.

prompt_templates.title: Check Types
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the lint skill to run the project typecheck and explain any type errors.

prompt_templates.title: Verify Release Changes
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the lint skill to lint relevant files, run typecheck, and identify any remaining verification gaps.

output_examples.input: Lint the files changed in this branch.
output_examples.output: The changed files were checked. Two unused imports were reported, and import order was fixed where the linter supported auto-fix.

output_examples.input: Run the typecheck after my refactor.
output_examples.output: The typecheck completed with one mismatch in a function return type. The affected area should be reviewed before merging.

output_examples.input: Clean up lint issues and tell me what remains.
output_examples.output: Auto-fix handled formatting and import order. One unused variable remains because it needs a code decision.

best_practices: Run lint and typecheck after functional changes, not only before release.
Review file diffs after auto-fix commands modify source files.
Adapt template commands to the repository package manager and language stack.
anti_patterns: Running auto-fix without reviewing the resulting source changes.
Assuming template commands work before checking project configuration.
Ignoring typecheck failures because lint passed.

faq.question: Does this skill install lint tools?
faq.answer: No. It only documents commands. The repository must already provide the required tools and dependencies.

faq.question: Can it fix lint problems automatically?
faq.answer: It includes an auto-fix command for tools that support fixes. Users should review all changed files afterward.

faq.question: Does it support TypeScript?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes a TypeScript no-emit typecheck command as the primary typecheck example.

faq.question: Can it support Python or Go projects?
faq.answer: It mentions ruff, mypy, golangci-lint, and go build as placeholders. Those commands may need project-specific edits.

faq.question: Is this safe to use in any repository?
faq.answer: Use it in trusted workspaces. It can run local project commands through Bash.

faq.question: What should I do after auto-fix runs?
faq.answer: Review the diff, confirm behavior is unchanged, and rerun lint or typecheck if needed.
